subject: Cloud Computing
title: The Strangler Fig Pattern
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Strangler Fig Pattern usable.

hook.text: A strangler fig tree grows around its host, slowly replacing it. That's how you migrate from a monolith.

buildup.text: Instead of rewriting everything at once, you build new services alongside the old system and route traffic gradually.

discovery.text: Feature by feature, the new system takes over. The old monolith shrinks until it can be safely removed.

twist.text: Many teams get stuck in the middle: running both old and new systems for years, paying double the cost.

climax.text: The strangler fig pattern is the safest migration path. Risky big-bang rewrites fail far more often.

punchline.text: Don't rewrite everything at once. Strangle the old system slowly.

quiz.question: What is the strangler fig pattern?
options: Gradually replacing a monolith by building new services alongside it
Shutting down the old system and launching a complete rewrite
Running two identical copies of the same system for redundancy
